notes: Hand-seeded status registry keyed by canonical manufacturer slug. AUDIT REQUIRED — dates and statuses need verification per vendor. Empty arrays are intentional (no known risk flags at seed time). last_verified = date Claude checked; should be refreshed regularly.

status_values.active_restricted: Operating but flagged (sanctions, export control, covered foreign entity)
status_values.supply_constrained: Active but chronic shortages or long lead times
status_values.defunct: Ceased operations, no longer shipping or supporting products
status_values.acquired: Absorbed into parent company; original brand may persist
status_values.eol: Still available but officially end-of-life, no successor from same vendor
status_values.unknown: Status not confirmed — use web search to verify

brain-fpv.name: Brain FPV
brain-fpv.notes: BrainFPV is ACTIVE. Ships RADIX 2 H7 FC manufactured in Oregon, USA. NDAA compliant (US-made). RADIX LI discontinued 2022 but RADIX 2 is current production. Website live at brainfpv.com. Previous "defunct" status was incorrect — based on RADIX LI EOL, not company closure.
brain-fpv.replacement_suggestion: matek-systems / holybro / speedybee F7 AIO class

brother-hobby.name: Brother Hobby
brother-hobby.notes: Active FPV motor manufacturer. Avenger, Returner, VY, Speed Dragon, LPD lines popular for 5"-10" freestyle and cinelifter builds.

t-motor.name: T-Motor
t-motor.notes: Large motor manufacturer spanning FPV to heavy lift. F-series, P-series, MN-series, Velox. NDAA-compliant line (Blue UAS Framework listed for select SKUs).

iflight.name: iFlight
iflight.notes: BumbleBee, Nazgul, Chimera, DC series frames; Xing motors; BLITZ ESC/FC line; SucceX FCs.

foxeer.name: Foxeer
foxeer.notes: Analog FPV cameras (Razer, Predator, Toothless) and antennas (Lollipop, Echo).

runcam.name: RunCam
runcam.notes: Analog + HD FPV cameras; Phoenix, Racer, Night Eagle analog; Link ecosystem for HD.

happymodel.name: Happymodel
happymodel.notes: Mobula, Crux, Moblite tinywhoop/micro class; ELRS integration.

betafpv.name: BetaFPV
betafpv.notes: Meteor/Pavo whoops; also has a Meteor Pro NDAA line marketed for defense FPV.

flywoo.name: Flywoo
flywoo.notes: Explorer LR, Mr. Croc, Firefly product lines.

axisflying.name: Axisflying
axisflying.notes: Motors and CineON / Manta cinewhoops. NDAA FPV line announced.

holybro.name: Holybro
holybro.notes: Kakute FC line, Tekko ESCs, Pix32/Pixhawk variants, Atlatl VTXes. Also sells ArduPilot/PX4 autopilots.

speedybee.name: SpeedyBee
speedybee.notes: Popular FC + ESC stacks with Bluetooth config. F7, F405, MasterV3 lines.

matek-systems.name: Matek Systems
matek-systems.notes: F722/H743 FCs preferred for long-range / iNav / ArduPilot builds. GPS modules (M10Q, M9N).

diatone.name: Diatone
diatone.notes: Roma, Taycan frames; Mamba FC/ESC stacks.

hglrc.name: HGLRC
hglrc.notes: Zeus FCs, Sector frames, Forward/Recon long-range builds.

walksnail.name: Walksnail (Caddx)
walksnail.notes: Avatar HD / Moonlight air units — one of two non-DJI HD analog-compatible HD video stacks. Caddx parent company.

hdzero.name: HDZero (Divimath)
hdzero.notes: Low-latency digital FPV. Race / Whoop / Freestyle VTXes. Open-source firmware. Operates between US and China.

dji.name: DJI
dji.notes: Covered foreign entity under ASDA / FAR 52.240-1. FCC Covered List (Dec 22 2025) categorically prohibits equipment authorization. Not NDAA-compliant. DJI O3/O4 air units, Mavic/Phantom/Matrice all affected for US use.
dji.replacement_suggestion: Skydio / Freefly / Teal / Inspired Flight for platforms; Walksnail or HDZero for HD VTX

autel.name: Autel Robotics
autel.notes: Named with DJI on FCC Covered List (Dec 22 2025). Not NDAA-compliant. EVO series platforms affected.

yuneec.name: Yuneec
yuneec.notes: Chinese-origin. FCC Covered List updated Dec 22 2025 to include ALL foreign-produced UAS and critical components. Yuneec is now explicitly covered. Blue UAS exemption applies until Jan 1 2027 for Blue UAS-listed systems only — Yuneec has no Blue UAS listing. Ineligible for new FCC authorizations, federal procurement, or grant-funded purchases.

parrot.name: Parrot
parrot.notes: French. ANAFI USA is Blue UAS Cleared. Distinct from the consumer ANAFI line.

freefly-systems.name: Freefly Systems
freefly-systems.notes: Alta X, Astro platforms. Industry-canonical §848 compliance PDFs. Both standard and Blue variants. See /spec-sheets/.

leadership.name: Tabb Firchau
leadership.title: Founder / CEO
leadership.background: Founded Freefly in 2011 as a camera-motion company (MoVI gimbals for Hollywood). Pivoted the company toward heavy-lift UAS with Alta 6 and Alta X. Engineering-led culture; Freefly's §848 compliance rigor comes directly from his approach.

skydio.name: Skydio
skydio.notes: X10D defense variant on Blue UAS Cleared List. X2, X10 consumer/enterprise lines. Exited the consumer market in 2023 to focus on enterprise and defense — pivotal strategic move.

leadership.name: Adam Bry
leadership.title: Co-founder / CEO
leadership.background: Co-founded Skydio in 2014 with two MIT PhDs (Pieter Abbeel's robotics lab adjacent). Previously at Google X's Project Wing. The 2023 consumer-exit to focus on enterprise/defense was his call — made Skydio the flagship US-made NDAA drone company.

leadership.name: Abraham Bachrach
leadership.title: Co-founder / CTO
leadership.background: Co-founded with Bry. Autonomy / computer vision technical lead. Skydio's obstacle-avoidance IP comes from his research.

red-cat.name: Red Cat Holdings / Teal Drones
red-cat.notes: Teal 2 / Black Widow. Blue UAS Cleared. US Army SRR program winner (Short Range Reconnaissance). Publicly traded (NASDAQ: RCAT).

leadership.name: Jeff Thompson
leadership.title: CEO
leadership.background: Led Red Cat Holdings through the Teal Drones acquisition and the pivot to defense. Drove the company to the SRR Program win, one of the most consequential US Army drone contracts.

inspired-flight.name: Inspired Flight Technologies
inspired-flight.notes: IF750A / IF800 / IF1200A Blue UAS Cleared platforms.

doodle-labs.name: Doodle Labs
doodle-labs.notes: Helix Mesh Rider — Blue UAS Framework. AUVSI Green UAS certified.

silvus.name: Silvus Technologies
silvus.notes: StreamCaster MANET radios (LITE 4200, 4400). AUVSI Green UAS certified. Defense-focused.

modalai.name: ModalAI
modalai.notes: VOXL 2, Sentinel, Starling 2. Blue UAS Framework. Snapdragon-based combined FC + companion computer. Spun out of Qualcomm's drone business. One of the most technically advanced US autonomy stacks.

leadership.name: Chad Sweet
leadership.title: CEO
leadership.background: Ex-Qualcomm drone division lead. Carried the Snapdragon Flight IP into ModalAI when Qualcomm wound down the division. Technical depth in onboard AI inference and optical-flow navigation.

ark-electronics.name: ARK Electronics
ark-electronics.notes: Blue UAS Framework PX4/ArduPilot flight controllers. US-manufactured.

auterion.name: Auterion
auterion.notes: Skynode S autopilot — Blue UAS Framework. PX4-based enterprise autopilot.

cubepilot.name: CubePilot
cubepilot.notes: Cube Orange+, Cube Black, Herelink datalink. Australian — TAA-eligible but not Blue UAS cleared.

raspberry-pi.name: Raspberry Pi Foundation / Trading
raspberry-pi.notes: Chronic supply constraints through 2022-2024 with industrial / commercial allocation priority. RPi 5 launched 2023, eased some pressure. CM4 availability still lumpy.
raspberry-pi.replacement_suggestion: Radxa (Rock 5B, CM3, CM5); Pine64 SOQuartz (CM4 drop-in); Orange Pi 5

radxa.name: Radxa
radxa.notes: Rock 5B (RK3588), Rock 5C, CM3 (RPi CM4 pin-compatible drop-in), CM5 (RPi CM5 pin-compatible). Preferred substitute during RPi shortages.
radxa.use_case: Drop-in alternative for Raspberry Pi CM4/CM5 and RPi 5 form factors

nvidia-jetson.name: NVIDIA Jetson
nvidia-jetson.notes: Jetson Nano (original) is EOL; Orin Nano / Orin NX / AGX Orin are current. Preferred for AI-inference on drones.

tbs.name: Team BlackSheep
tbs.notes: Crossfire + Tracer long-range control links, Unify VTXes, Fusion. Pioneered long-range FPV.

radiomaster.name: RadioMaster
radiomaster.notes: TX16S, Boxer, Pocket, Zorro transmitters. Multi-protocol (ELRS, Crossfire, FrSky).

frsky.name: FrSky
frsky.notes: Chinese-origin RC/radio manufacturer. FCC Covered List Dec 22 2025 covers UAS critical components from foreign countries — FrSky receivers/transmitters used in UAS may be affected. Still shipping but declining FPV mindshare vs ExpressLRS/Crossfire. For new defense/federal builds, specify ExpressLRS or CRSF alternatives.

trurc.name: TrueRC
trurc.notes: X-AIR, X2-AIR, Singularity patch antennas. Canada — TAA-eligible.

orqa.name: ORQA
orqa.notes: FPV.One goggles line. Acquired ImmersionRC (~2023), rolling Tramp VTX and Ghost RC protocol into a full-stack NDAA-compliant FPV ecosystem: FPV.One goggles + FC2020-F722 HD flight controller + ESC2020 + IRONGhost datalink (built on Ghost RC). Only purpose-built NDAA-compliant FPV stack in market. EU manufacture (Croatia).

leadership.name: Srdjan Kovacevic
leadership.title: Co-founder / CEO
leadership.background: Croatian entrepreneur. Led ORQA from FPV.One goggles into the full NDAA-compliant FPV ecosystem thesis. The IRC acquisition was his strategic bet on owning an entire vertical stack outside China.

immersionrc.name: ImmersionRC (now part of ORQA)
immersionrc.notes: Legendary FPV vendor — Vortex racers, Tramp HV VTX, Ghost RC control link protocol. Acquired by ORQA circa 2023. Products continue to ship but under the ORQA ecosystem umbrella. Tramp and Ghost tech are the foundation of ORQA's IRONGhost datalink.

unusual-machines.name: Unusual Machines, Inc. (UMAC)
unusual-machines.notes: Publicly-traded (NYSE American: UMAC). US-made NDAA-compliant FPV components. Full subsidiary tree: (1) Brave FPV — Brave F7/F7+ FC (first FPV FC on Blue UAS Framework, Aug 2024), Brave ESC; (2) Fat Shark — Aura analog camera, Aura VTX, HDO+ goggles (acquired Feb 2024 from Red Cat); (3) Rotor Riot — e-commerce channel (acquired Feb 2024 from Red Cat); (4) Rotor Lab — motor production (acquired July 2025, Australian, now producing in Orlando FL). FY2025: $11.2M revenue (101% YoY), $103M cash, $157M raised in 2025. Key defense orders: PDW $3.75M (Dec 2025), Strategic Logix $12.8M (RRSL program), Red Cat FANG, Army PBAS Tranche 1.1 customers. Stock: ~$13.70 (Apr 2026). Donald Trump Jr. joined board 2024.

rotor-riot.name: Rotor Riot (now Unusual Machines)
rotor-riot.notes: FPV retailer founded by Chad Kapper / Steele Davis / early FPV racing scene. Acquired by Unusual Machines early 2024. Continues to operate as the retail/brand arm of the parent. Brave FPV motor/ESC/FC line is sold through Rotor Riot and is a candidate for NDAA-compliant US manufacturing.

fat-shark.name: Fat Shark (now Unusual Machines)
fat-shark.notes: Original FPV goggle brand. Dominion / Attitude / Recon lines. Acquired by Unusual Machines 2024 alongside Rotor Riot. A US-made Fat Shark HD line is UMAC's plausible NDAA goggle play — watch for announcements.

hylio.name: Hylio
hylio.notes: AG-122, AG-230, AG-272 agricultural spray UAS. Most thorough public compliance disclosure pattern (§848 + §889 + §817 + ASDA + sub-vendor attestations).

draganfly.name: Draganfly
draganfly.notes: Draganfly Inc (DPRO) is a publicly-traded Canadian/US company — confirmed operating as of 2025-2026. The "acquired 2020" note in entity graph likely refers to a subsidiary or asset acquisition, not company shutdown. Status: active. NDAA compliance posture: US-listed company, verify component sourcing before federal procurement.

expresslrs.name: ExpressLRS
expresslrs.notes: Open-source project, NOT a company. Hardware is made by many vendors (HappyModel, BetaFPV, Matek, Radiomaster, etc.). No single point of failure.

anzu_robotics.name: Anzu Robotics
anzu_robotics.notes: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (Shenzhen DJI Technology Co., Ltd.) | Teardown confirms adversary hardware: PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise. Board part numbers match. C

cogito_specta.name: Cogito Tech / SPECTA
cogito_specta.notes: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (undisclosed — no licensing acknowledged) | Teardown confirms adversary hardware: Specta Air has identical PCBs to DJI Air 3. Same DJI P1 chipset. RF emission repor

skyrover_knowact.name: SkyRover / Knowact
skyrover_knowact.notes: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (undisclosed) | 100% of components sourced from adversary nations

autel_robotics.name: Autel Robotics
autel_robotics.notes: Technology licensed from adversary entity: Proprietary (not DJI derivative) | 90% of components sourced from adversary nations

brave-fpv.name: Brave FPV
brave-fpv.notes: Brand owned by Unusual Machines (UMAC). Produces Brave F7 and F7+ flight controllers — first FPV FCs on the DIU Blue UAS Framework (listed Aug 7 2024, sponsored by Marine Corps Warfighting Lab). Also produces Brave ESC. US-manufactured in Orlando FL. NDAA compliant. 7,000+ Brave F7 units ordered within first week of Blue UAS listing. Components used in Red Cat FANG, Strategic Logix RRSL, and Army PBAS programs.
